things.name: Kazan Kremlin (UNESCO)
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage fortress at the confluence of the Kazanka and Volga rivers; the only Tatar kremlin in existence, blending Orthodox and Islamic architecture

things.name: Kul Sharif Mosque
things.description: Magnificent blue-domed mosque inside the Kazan Kremlin, rebuilt in 2005 on the site of the 16th-century original destroyed by Ivan the Terrible; one of Russia's largest mosques

things.name: Annunciation Cathedral
things.description: Magnificent 16th-century Orthodox cathedral inside the Kremlin built after Ivan the Terrible's conquest of Kazan in 1552; a symbol of the city's dual Christian and Muslim identity

things.name: Bauman Street
things.description: Kazan's bustling pedestrian boulevard lined with 18th and 19th century architecture, street performers and cafes; the social heart of the city linking the Kremlin to the lake

things.name: National Museum of Tatarstan
things.description: Comprehensive museum of Tatar history and culture with extraordinary collections of Tatar gold jewellery, 16th-century armour and exhibits on the Golden Horde

things.name: Kazan Federal University
things.description: One of Russia's oldest universities founded in 1804 where Lenin and Tolstoy studied; the magnificent main building is a national monument and key part of Kazan's heritage

things.name: Kazan Family Centre (Wedding Palace)
things.description: Kazan's extraordinary landmark nicknamed the Cauldron or Cheburashka; a spectacular modern building shaped like a cauldron hosting the city registry office

things.name: Old Tatar Quarter (Stara Tatarskaya Sloboda)
things.description: Historic neighbourhood preserving traditional Tatar wooden architecture, small mosques and the Mardjani Mosque from 1767; the authentic heart of Tatar cultural life in Kazan

things.name: Kazan Zoo
things.description: One of Russia's finest zoos set in a park overlooking the Kazanka River; home to over 3,000 animals and popular with families visiting this modern facility

things.name: Kazan Arena
things.description: Spectacular 45,379-seat stadium hosting 2018 World Cup matches; home of Rubin Kazan, twice champions of Russia (2008, 2009) and UEFA Champions League group participants in 2010-11
